ab: ka 2022 a cavilje a tja sihu ljemaui tu makayanga akipavecik tua tjia kinacalisianan a ngadan pita nibonsiu.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: na kiaivuan aken tu na kisamulja cuacuai a tji sulja pakata venecik a pita nibonsiu a kinacalisian a ngadan.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年sulja。
en: I interviewed sulja, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: na mareaivu amen. tu micevun nu niciu a mata uma’ nji madu.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: ka tazua, pasemalauvin a ken tu a na kemasinu sa ngada nji madju sa siukai tua matjaljaljak kata inljan nji madju.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: kivada’ aken nakuya a tja sikivecikan na tjucu aku sun a kiaun tu paumaljin?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: temevela tji madju, a ljinautjian na vinecikan ini’ ka makaya lemuluai tua ku kinacalisianangn a ngadan mapasaliukamaya liau a napasamli sa sikapedjelian.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「舒先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Shu Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: na ivu tji sulja a kinacalisianang a sivecikan pa’ulid tu njitjen na ngadan. njitjen na zukuan tja kina cemekeljan atja djmu’ ulja na makaya a tjatjuruvu a caucau a kipavecik tuatja kinacalisianang a ngadan.
ch: sulja說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: cemelesai said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
